One of my favourite movies of all time is "Autografia" by Miguel Mendes. It’s a documentary about Mário Cesariny, a portuguese poet and painter. I’ve watched this movie many times, in many formats and I hold it very dear and close to my heart.
There’s something Cesariny says that I will never forget and I think about it all the time. When talking about why he doesn’t write poetry anymore, he confesses:

“I don’t write verses just because. (…) Don’t think I don’t miss those times. When I felt like writing, and I’d go to cafes and walk around on the street. I never wrote a poem at home. Never! Don’t ask me why. On the streets… It was like flying. Now it’s gone.”
There was a time when I'd write on a little pocket notebook while walking because I wanted to fly like him.  And it worked! You can really fly like that. I got a new pocket notebook.
A year ago I was in lisbon and there was a Cesariny exhibition at the MAAT and I got to see his iconic hat from the documentary. I was so happy. https://t.co/BLuZL2bvMl
